Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire

Class KeyExpr

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire.KeyExpr
public final class KeyExpr extends Object
Zenoh key expression: a string with wildcard semantics matching the spec at zenoh.io/docs/manual/abstractions.

Wildcard syntax (chunk-based, / separators)

  • * — a whole chunk. E.g. a/*&#47;c matches a/anything/c but not a/x/y/c.
  • ** — zero or more chunks. E.g. a/**&#47;c matches a/c, a/x/c, a/x/y/c, etc.
  • $* — sub-chunk wildcard, i.e. [^/]* inside a single chunk. E.g. sensor$*&#47;temp matches sensor1/temp and sensorABC/temp. Chunk-only ($* as a whole chunk) is non-canonical — use * instead.

Operations

  • matches(String) — does this KE match a concrete (wildcard-free) key? Used subscriber-side after receiving a PUT whose key is the resolved concrete key.
  • intersects(KeyExpr) — is there ANY concrete key that would match both this and the argument? Used router-side for subscription routing and client-side for "does my declared filter apply to this incoming publisher's key expression?". Symmetric.

Wildcard intersection is a faithful port of the reference ClassicIntersector in commons/zenoh-keyexpr/src/key_expr/intersect/classical.rs. The STAR_DSL=true variant (which recognises $*) is the one routers use.

Verbatim / admin-space chunks (leading @) are NOT specially handled in this v1 — admin-space subscribers are a follow-up. The classical intersector would refuse to match verbatim chunks against wildcards for safety; we tolerate them (match on equality) since the common case is subscriber-side wildcards against non-verbatim keys.

    True iff there exists any concrete key that matches BOTH this KE and other.

    True iff this key expression contains no wildcards (no * or $).

    Does this key expression match the given concrete (wildcard-free) key? The argument MUST be a concrete key; passing another wildcard KE here is a bug — use intersects(KeyExpr) for wildcard-vs-wildcard.

  • Method Details

    • value

      public String value()

    • of

      public static KeyExpr of(String s)
      Sugar for new KeyExpr(s).

    • isConcrete

      public boolean isConcrete()
      True iff this key expression contains no wildcards (no * or $).

    • matches

      public boolean matches(String concreteKey)
      Does this key expression match the given concrete (wildcard-free) key? The argument MUST be a concrete key; passing another wildcard KE here is a bug — use intersects(KeyExpr) for wildcard-vs-wildcard.

      Behaviour: equivalent to intersects(new KeyExpr(concrete)) but slightly cheaper on the fast path since we know one side has no wildcards.

    • intersects

      public boolean intersects(KeyExpr other)
      True iff there exists any concrete key that matches BOTH this KE and other. Symmetric.

      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • resolveKey

      public static String resolveKey(String org, String keyExpr)
      Prepend org to keyExpr with slash normalisation.

      Behaviour must remain byte-identical to io.mdudel.zenoh.ZenohClient.resolveKey(String, String) in the JNI-backed sibling module — the two implementations are meant to be drop-in swappable, which requires the effective wire key be the same for the same inputs. If the JNI version's behaviour changes, this must change with it.

      Examples:

         resolveKey(null,   "tracks") -> "tracks"
   resolveKey("",     "tracks") -> "tracks"
   resolveKey("acme", "tracks") -> "acme/tracks"
   resolveKey("acme/","tracks") -> "acme/tracks"
   resolveKey("acme","/tracks") -> "acme/tracks"
   resolveKey("acme/","/tracks")-> "acme/tracks"
   resolveKey("acme",  "")      -> "acme"

Rebuilt from commit 2d2e383 at 2026-07-12 15:42 UTC
